title: Dermatology medication monitoring

medication.text: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.
medication.rationale: NG5 supports clinician-led review of medicine indication, effectiveness, safety, adherence, monitoring, patient concerns, and documented decisions; the broad dermatology workflow remains condition- and medicine-specific in practice.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

medication.text: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.
medication.rationale: Medicine-related communication across care settings was reviewed for documentation context.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

sources_used.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation

source_limitations: Condition, medicine, dose, duration, indication, response, adherence, adverse effects, interactions, pregnancy potential, required laboratory or clinical monitoring, results, clinician decision, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
